cover.headline_html: 3 myths that kill GovCon entries before they start.

myths.label: The Past-Performance Trap
myths.myth_text: You need past performance to win any federal contract.
myths.reality_text: Teaming agreements let new firms piggyback on a prime's track record. Mentor-protégé programs exist for this exact reason. GSA Schedule approval doesn't even require federal past performance.

myths.label: The Price War Lie
myths.myth_text: You have to be the cheapest option to win federal work.
myths.reality_text: Federal contracts are evaluated on best value — not just price. Technical approach, experience, and reliability all factor in. Undercutting on price often signals low quality and tanks your margins.

myths.label: The Big-Company Lock-Out
myths.myth_text: The GSA Schedule is really built for large businesses.
myths.reality_text: Over 80% of GSA Schedule holders are small businesses. Congress mandates 23% of all federal contracting dollars go to small businesses. The system was designed with you in mind.

close.close_question: Drop the myth you believed when you first started exploring GovCon.

template_data.image_prompt: Split-panel infographic cover. Dark navy background. Bold centered headline: '3 MYTHS KILLING YOUR GOVCON ENTRY'. Three horizontal rows below, each with a red X icon and a short 3-word myth label. Right side: illustrated professional woman in blazer, confident stance. Coral accent on word 'MYTHS'. Footer text: 'Josh Ladick · GSA Focus'.
